title: Caves of Qud - A Digital Dive Let's Play and Discussion
body:
Daniel (@Dungeondive) dives into the fascinating, complex world of Caves of Qud, a roguelike RPG known for its depth, procedurally generated content, and rich, weird-fiction-inspired lore. Newly released in its 1.0 version, Caves of Qud is a blend of story-driven quests and open-world exploration, featuring simulated politics, factions, and religions. The game randomly generates ancient histories, creating a dynamic experience for every playthrough.

Daniel explores the game as a beginner, starting with character creation, showcasing its detailed system reminiscent of GURPS or Champions. The journey begins in the village of Joppa, where initial quests set the stage for discovery, combat, and a chance to learn the intricacies of this captivating world. Encounters with ancient shrines, complex reputations among factions, and an array of weird and wonderful artifacts showcase the richness of Qud’s setting.

Fans of Michael Shea, Jack Vance, and Clark Ashton Smith will appreciate the literary influences, while tabletop enthusiasts may find parallels to games like Into the Odd, Vaults of Vaarn, and even Gamma World.

If you enjoy intricate, systems-driven games and love a challenge, Caves of Qud might just be your next great adventure.
